headline: Survey at 30% target (403/1,350). Strong regional spread but urgent acceleration needed.
collection_status: Currently tracking at 29.9% of target with 403 responses across all 10 regions. Lindi dominates with 80 responses (19.9% of total), while Rukwa severely underrepresented at 9 responses (2.2%). Current pace suggests ~24 weeks to target; acceleration by 40-50% required to meet deadline.
key_findings: Teacher engagement strong: 270 KiuFunza teachers (67% of respondents) indicate programme embedding; 159 lower primary teachers lead participation.
Expansion priority dominates: 144 respondents (35.7%) prioritise expanding KiuFunza over all other outcomes; new programmes (23.3%) and policy integration (20.8%) follow.
Scale readiness concern: 2.7/5 average readiness score signals significant capability gaps despite 4.4/5 partner satisfaction—implementation infrastructure lags stakeholder confidence.
Policy and government partnership critical: Feedback consistently emphasises government integration, digital monitoring systems, and sustained teacher incentives as prerequisites for scale.
flags: Rukwa (9 responses) and Singida (25) critically undersampled; risk skewed regional insights if not addressed within 2 weeks.
Scale readiness (2.7/5) versus partner satisfaction gap (4.4/5) suggests stakeholders back KiuFunza but doubt operational capacity—threatens expansion credibility.
Only 8 responses on research (2% priority weight) indicates weak appetite for evidence generation; may compromise learning from scale effort.
recommendation: Immediately mobilise regional coordinators to intensify collection in Rukwa, Singida, Songwe, and Manyara (currently 126 combined, need 180+). Simultaneously conduct targeted follow-up interviews with 15-20 respondents on scale readiness barriers to diagnose infrastructure gaps before expansion planning.
